# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { openKv } from "https://deno.land/std@0.123.0/flag/mod.ts";
import type { KvOptions } from "https://deno.land/std@0.123.0/kv/mod.ts";

const kv = await Deno.openKv({
  name: 'edgeDB',
  dir: './data',
});

async function main() {
  const [method, url] = Deno.args;

  if (method !== 'GET') {
    console.error('Only GET requests are allowed');
    return;
  }

  if (!url.startsWith('/search')) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid route: ${url}`);
  }

  try {
    const params = new URLSearchParams(url.split('?')[1]);
    const filterParam = params.get('filter');

    let key: string;
    if (filterParam) {
      key = ['users', 'all', filterParam];
    } else {
      key = ['users'];
    }

    const users = await kv.get(key.join('-'));
    if (!users) {
      console.log({ message: 'No results found' });
      return;
    }

    const filteredUsers = JSON.parse(users).filter((user: any) => {
      for (const param in filterParam) {
        if (filterParam[param] && user[param] !== filterParam[param]) {
          return false;
        }
      }
      return true;
    });

    console.log(filteredUsers);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error(err);
  }
}

main();
